esp32.wake_on_touch.description: Configure whether or not a touch will wake the device from sleep. wake should be a boolean value.

esp32.wake_on_ulp.description: Configure whether or not the Ultra-Low-Power co-processor can wake the device from sleep. wake should be a boolean value.

esp32.wake_on_ext0.description: Configure how EXT0 wakes the device from sleep. pin can be None or a valid Pin object. level should be esp32.WAKEUP_ALL_LOW or esp32.WAKEUP_ANY_HIGH.

esp32.wake_on_ext1.description: Configure how EXT1 wakes the device from sleep. pins can be None or a tuple/list of valid Pin objects. level should be esp32.WAKEUP_ALL_LOW or esp32.WAKEUP_ANY_HIGH.

esp32.wake_on_gpio.description: Configure how GPIO wakes the device from sleep. pins can be None or a tuple/list of valid Pin objects. level should be esp32.WAKEUP_ALL_LOW or esp32.WAKEUP_ANY_HIGH.

esp32.gpio_deep_sleep_hold.description: Configure whether non-RTC GPIO pin configuration is retained during deep-sleep mode for held pads. enable should be a boolean value.

esp32.raw_temperature.description: Read the raw value of the internal temperature sensor, returning an integer.

esp32.idf_heap_info.description: Returns information about the ESP-IDF heap memory regions. One of them contains the MicroPython heap and the others are used by ESP-IDF, e.g., for network buffers and other data. This data is useful to get a sense of how much memory is available to ESP-IDF and the networking stack in particular. It may shed some light on situations where ESP-IDF operations fail due to allocation failures.

esp32.idf_task_info.description: Returns information about running ESP-IDF/FreeRTOS tasks, which include MicroPython threads. This data is useful to gain insight into how much time tasks spend running or if they are blocked for significant parts of time, and to determine if allocated stacks are fully utilized or might be reduced.

esp32.Partition.description: Create an object representing a partition. id can be a string which is the label of the partition to retrieve, or one of the constants: BOOT or RUNNING. block_size specifies the byte size of an individual block.

esp32.Partition.find.description: Find a partition specified by type, subtype and label. Returns a (possibly empty) list of Partition objects. Note: subtype=0xff matches any subtype and label=None matches any label.

esp32.Partition.info.description: Returns a 6-tuple (type, subtype, addr, size, label, encrypted).

esp32.Partition.ioctl.description: These methods implement the simple and extended block protocol defined by vfs.AbstractBlockDev.

esp32.Partition.set_boot.description: Sets the partition as the boot partition.

esp32.Partition.get_next_update.description: Gets the next update partition after this one, and returns a new Partition object. Typical usage is Partition(Partition.RUNNING).get_next_update() which returns the next partition to update given the current running one.

esp32.Partition.mark_app_valid_cancel_ro.description: Signals that the current boot is considered successful. Calling mark_app_valid_cancel_rollback is required on the first boot of a new partition to avoid an automatic rollback at the next boot. This uses the ESP-IDF “app rollback” feature with “CONFIG_BOOTLOADER_APP_ROLLBACK_ENABLE” and an OSError(-261) is raised if called on firmware that doesn’t have the feature enabled. It is OK to call mark_app_valid_cancel_rollback on every boot and it is not necessary when booting firmware that was loaded using esptool.

esp32.Partition.BOOT.description: Used in the Partition constructor to fetch various partitions: BOOT is the partition that will be booted at the next reset and RUNNING is the currently running partition.

esp32.Partition.TYPE_APP.description: Used in Partition.find to specify the partition type: APP is for bootable firmware partitions (typically labelled factory, ota_0, ota_1), and DATA is for other partitions, e.g. nvs, otadata, phy_init, vfs.

esp32.PCNT.description: Returns the singleton PCNT instance for the given unit id.

esp32.PCNT.init.description: (Re-)initialise a pulse counter unit. Supported keyword arguments are:

esp32.PCNT.value.description: Call this method with no arguments to return the current counter value.

esp32.PCNT.irq.description: ESP32 pulse counters support interrupts on these counter events:

esp32.RMT.signature: class esp32.RMT(channel, *, pin=None, resolution_hz=10000000, clock_div=None, idle_level=False, num_symbols=48 | 64, tx_carrier=None)
esp32.RMT.description: This class provides access to one of the eight RMT channels. channel is optional and a dummy parameter for backward compatibility. pin is required and configures which Pin is bound to the RMT channel. resolution_hz defines the resolution/unit of the samples. For example, 1,000,000 means the unit is microsecond. The pulse widths can assume values up to RMT.PULSE_MAX, so the resolution should be selected accordingly to the signal to be transmitted. clock_div (deprecated) is equivalent to resolution_hz, but expressed as a clock divider that divides the source clock (80MHz) to the RMT channel allowing the resolution to be specified. Either clock_div and resolution_hz may be supplied, but not both. num_symbols specifies the RMT buffer allocated for this channel (minimum 48 or 64, depending on chip), from a small pool of symbols (192 to 512, depending on chip) that are shared by all channels. This buffer does not limit the size of the pulse train that you can send, but bigger buffers reduce the CPU load and the potential of glitches/imprecise pulse lengths. idle_level specifies what level the output will be when no transmission is in progress and can be any value that converts to a boolean, with True representing high voltage and False representing low.

esp32.RMT.source_freq.description: Returns the source clock frequency. Currently the source clock is not configurable so this will always return 80MHz.

esp32.RMT.clock_div.description: Return the clock divider. Note that the channel resolution is 1 / (source_freq / clock_div). (Method deprecated. The value may not be faithful if resolution was supplied as resolution_hz.)

esp32.RMT.wait_done.description: Returns True if the channel is idle or False if a sequence of pulses started with RMT.write_pulses is being transmitted. If the timeout keyword argument is given then block for up to this many milliseconds for transmission to complete. Timeout of -1 blocks until transmission is complete (and blocks forever if loop is enabled).

esp32.RMT.loop.description: Configure looping on the channel. enable_loop is bool, set to True to enable looping on the next call to RMT.write_pulses. If called with False while a looping sequence is currently being transmitted then the transmission will stop. (Method deprecated by RMT.loop_count.)

esp32.RMT.loop_count.description: Configure looping on the channel. n is int. Affects the next call to RMT.write_pulses. Set to 0 to disable looping, -1 to enable infinite looping, or a positive number to loop for a given number of times. If n is changed, the current transmission is stopped.

esp32.RMT.active.description: If called without parameters, returns True if there is an ongoing transmission.

esp32.RMT.deinit.description: Release all RMT resources and invalidate the object. All subsequent method calls will raise OSError. Useful to free RMT resources without having to wait for the object to be garbage-collected.

esp32.RMT.write_pulses.description: Begin transmitting a sequence. There are three ways to specify this:

esp32.RMT.bitstream_rmt.description: Configure RMT usage in the machine.bitstream implementation.

esp32.RMT.bitstream_channel.description: This function is deprecated and will be replaced by `RMT.bitstream_rmt()`.

esp32.RMT.PULSE_MAX.description: Maximum integer that can be set for a pulse duration.

esp32.ULP.description: This class provides access to the Ultra-Low-Power co-processor.

esp32.ULP.load_binary.description: Load a program_binary into the ULP at the given load_addr.

esp32.ULP.run.description: Start the ULP running at the given entry_point.

esp32.esp32.WAKEUP_ALL_LOW.description: Selects the wake level for pins.

esp32.NVS.description: Create an object providing access to a namespace (which is automatically created if not present).

esp32.NVS.set_i32.description: Sets a 32-bit signed integer value for the specified key. Remember to call commit!

esp32.NVS.get_i32.description: Returns the signed integer value for the specified key. Raises an OSError if the key does not exist or has a different type.

esp32.NVS.set_blob.description: Sets a binary blob value for the specified key. The value passed in must support the buffer protocol, e.g. bytes, bytearray, str. (Note that esp-idf distinguishes blobs and strings, this method always writes a blob even if a string is passed in as value.) Remember to call commit!

esp32.NVS.get_blob.description: Reads the value of the blob for the specified key into the buffer, which must be a bytearray. Returns the actual length read. Raises an OSError if the key does not exist, has a different type, or if the buffer is too small.

esp32.NVS.commit.description: Commits changes made by set_xxx methods to flash.

esp32.LDO.description: Create an object providing access to the Low Dropout Voltage Regulator for a given LDO channel.

esp32.LDO.adjust_voltage.description: Adjust the voltage of the LDO channel.

esp.deepsleep.description: Note: ESP8266 only - use machine.deepsleep() on ESP32

esp.flash_size.description: Read the total size of the flash memory.

esp.flash_user_start.description: Read the memory offset at which the user flash space begins.
